title: Build-kind / exec vocabulary — which backlog kinds count as a "build" for the governance gates, after the kind-axis migration
summary: Prep research for decision #1473. THE PROBLEM: the backlog-health audit's exec gate (which kinds gate G2 built-ahead-of-ruling and G3 ungoverned-architecture) hardcodes `const isExec = it.type === 'idea' || it.type === 'issue'` (we:scripts/audit-backlog-health.mjs:334). The kinds `idea`/`issue` were REMOVED in the kind-axis migration (the old `type` axis was renamed to `kind`, and the kind set collapsed to story/decision/task/epic — see we:scripts/check-standards-rules.mjs:28 BACKLOG_KINDS). So `isExec` is ALWAYS false and G2/G3 silently never fire; the commit e9c6fca fixed only the FIELD read (fm.type → fm.kind ?? fm.type at :102), not the PREDICATE. THE CANONICAL RULE ALREADY EXISTS, in three consistent homes: (1) we:scripts/readiness/proposer.mjs:38 `const isBuildable = (it) => it.kind !== 'decision'` — the proposer drafts a next-build for every NON-decision kind (story/task build leaves, epic the umbrella); (2) we:scripts/check-standards-rules.mjs:34 `export const isExecKind = (kind) => kind !== 'decision'`, authored beside BACKLOG_KINDS with the explicit comment that it is 'the canonical form of proposer.mjs's isBuildable and the backlog-health audit's G2/G3 exec gate'; (3) we:scripts/__tests__/exec-kind.test.mjs pins isExecKind (covers every non-decision kind incl. epic, excludes decision, auto-covers a hypothetical new build kind, and guards the only silent-death vector — `decision` being renamed). So the repair is NOT greenfield: it is wiring the audit's dead predicate to consume the rule the rest of the repo already shares. EXTERNAL PRIOR ART (why a NON-decision allowlist beats an idea/issue enumeration): the failure mode is a hardcoded enumeration drifting silently when the vocabulary changes — the same class linters guard with a 'closed enum + a test that fails when a new member appears' (TypeScript exhaustiveness via `never`, ESLint no-fallthrough), and the `!== 'decision'` form is the open-complement equivalent: a NEW build kind is auto-covered and the only failure vector (renaming `decision`) is pinned by a test. The dead-enum drift here is exactly the silent-death the comment at check-standards-rules.mjs:32-41 calls out. THE EPIC-INCLUSION EDGE (the one genuine small call): should the exec gate treat ALL non-decision kinds (story/task/epic) as builds, or NARROW to leaf builds (story/task) and exclude epic umbrellas? Grounded against the real tree: 121 epics total, 37 resolved with graduatedTo set; of those, 6 graduate directly to a NAMED STANDARD ENTITY — #049→block:component, #351→project:webcompliance, #468→project:webblocks, #570→project:webcharts, #618→project:webediting, #1023→project:webreporting. A skeptic attack argued 'exclude epics — they double-count their children's graduations, losing zero coverage.' TESTED AGAINST DATA, that claim is mostly false: only 2 of the 6 are duplicated by a child carrying the SAME entity noun (#351↔#436 project:webcompliance, #618↔#629 project:webediting); the other 4 are UNIQUE to the epic — #049 block:component (children are file-paths/decisions), #468 project:webblocks (children carry DIFFERENT nouns block:checkbox/toggle/radio, never project:webblocks), #570 project:webcharts (children carry no graduation), #1023 project:webreporting (child is a demo file-path). Excluding epics would therefore BLIND G3 to project:webblocks/webcharts/webreporting and block:component — exactly the highest-value 'a whole new project/block shipped' graduations, which legitimately live at the epic level (a project IS naturally an epic, not a leaf story). The double-count is real but small (2/6) and is a SUBJECT-dedup problem (entity dedup across the parent/child closure — #1498's axis), not a reason to narrow the KIND gate; narrowing the kind axis to fix a subject-axis duplicate sacrifices 4 unique signals to suppress 2 harmless duplicates (the leaf still fires, so no signal is lost by keeping the epic). G2 is unaffected by epics regardless: G2 guards `if (!isDecision(p)) continue` (we:scripts/audit-backlog-health.mjs:356), so an epic's blockedBy edges to non-decisions never misfire. RECOMMENDATION handed to #1473: Fork 1 → consume the canonical isExecKind IMPORTED from check-standards-rules.mjs (single source already used by proposer + test; the import is acyclic and side-effect-free — verified), passing the kind value (`it.type`, which the audit already computes as `fm.kind ?? fm.type`). Fork 2 → include epics (all non-decision), routing the 2-case double-count to a subject-dedup residual on the G3 SUBJECT scope (#1498), not the kind gate. IMPLEMENTATION CO-DEPENDENCY surfaced by this survey: the audit script today consumes NEITHER helper — G3 at :366 does not call isEntityGraduation despite #1498's resolved 'shipped this turn' list claiming it landed. So wiring isExecKind ALONE resurrects G3 at full volume (~350 + epics); #1498's entity-subject scope must land in the SAME change or G3 is un-actionable. Classification: repo-internal backlog devtools/workflow tooling, NOT a WE standard (impl-is-not-a-standard, minimize-lock-in) — no protocol minted; the reusable rule ('the repo has ONE canonical build-kind predicate, kind !== decision, with a single importable home') is statute-layer material for platform-decisions.md / backlog-workflow.md on resolve. All numbers reproduced live from this tree; see reports/2026-06-22-build-kind-exec-vocabulary.md.
